scope.summary: Implement curvature editing operations on Panel EdgeShape outlines: promote a Line edge to Bezier, and expose control-handle drag (set_bezier_handles) as a CRDT-safe edit on the GarmentSpec panel
acceptance_criteria: promote_to_bezier(panel_id, edge_index) converts a Line EdgeShape to a Bezier with control handles initialized to the line endpoints (C1-continuous default)
set_bezier_handles(panel_id, edge_index, cp1: [f32;2], cp2: [f32;2]) updates the control handles in cm; emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded
PANEL_CLOSED ValidationDescriptor passes after any curvature edit
SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH re-evaluates correctly after Bezier edge shape change (arc-length integration, not chord)
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=set_bezier_handles
Bezier arc-length used in SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH and tessellation matches within 0.5mm tolerance
scope.risk_if_missed: The EdgeShape enum includes Bezier (MT-116) but without tooling to edit control handles there is no way for a model or operator to shape curved necklines, armholes, or waistlines. All curved pattern edges are left as piecewise-linear polygons, producing angular garment silhouettes that fail production quality standards.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
